title: Rewrite app with Linux-only ratatui runtime and typed job architecture
description:
## Summary
- Replace the legacy Windows-compatible TUI stack (~15k lines removed) with a lean Linux-only ratatui runtime built around a typed worker-job model, boot probes, and a `Cached<T>` state model.
- Add new domain modules (`probe`, `execute`, `llm`, `prompt`, `context`, `bookmark`, `status_store`) and screen modules (`landing`, `generate`, `status`) backed by a `runtime::{Runtime, jobs, cache}` layer.
- Delete the old monolithic `app.rs`, `generate.rs`, `ui.rs`, `jj.rs`, `llm.rs`, `prompt.rs`, `pull_requests.rs`, `repo.rs`, and `repo_options.rs`; add render-smoke tests for every new screen.

## Why
The previous implementation targeted Windows via `crossterm_winapi`, which caused unrecoverable TUI freezes and blocked instant-Esc support (kitty keyboard protocol). The rewrite drops Windows as a runtime target entirely, adopts a clean input-thread → owner-thread → worker-pool architecture, and replaces ad-hoc state with a `Cached<T>` model where `Loading` is only shown for in-flight user-visible fetches. Boot probes start before the first paint to eliminate cold-start latency.

## Verification
- Not run (not provided)
